humanSummary.text: Dashboard shell audit found no P0/P1 blockers. Pending-decision precision, unknown style/glossary states, and typed API error rendering are tracked as follow-up DAG work.

findings.title: Project-level findings can be hidden from pending decisions
findings.description: The dashboard derives pending decisions from locale-branch open finding counts plus runtime validation findings. Project-level findings without a locale branch can contribute to the project finding count while the prominent pending-decision band says no pending decisions.

evidence.detail: Audit observed pending decisions derived from branch/runtime counts while findings may be persisted without localeBranchId and still count at project level.
Dashboard, API handler, server, and typecheck focused tests passed during audit; this finding is non-blocking because current shell still surfaces aggregate project finding counts.

findings.impact: A reviewer may miss actionable project-level findings unless they notice lower-priority aggregate counts elsewhere on the page.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Dashboard pending-decision summaries include project-level findings that are not attached to a locale branch.
A dedicated API read model or equivalent typed response distinguishes locale-branch decisions, project-level decisions, and runtime validation decisions.
MSW/API-backed tests prove a project-level finding appears in the pending-decision area.

proposedDagNode.title: Dashboard decision read model
proposedDagNode.summary: Add a typed dashboard decision read model so pending decisions include project-level findings, locale-branch findings, and runtime validation decisions without inference drift.
deliverables: Decision read API or equivalent typed response
acceptanceCriteria: Project-level findings without localeBranchId appear in the dashboard pending-decision area
Locale-branch, project-level, and runtime validation decision counts are distinguishable in the read model
Dashboard tests prove no pending-decision band can contradict project finding counts

verification.value: pnpm exec vitest run apps/itotori/test/dashboard.test.ts apps/itotori/test/api-handlers.test.ts

findings.title: Style guide and glossary panels present unqueried state as empty
findings.description: The style-guide and glossary panels say no records were returned by the API even though the dashboard shell does not yet query a corresponding API response for those records.

evidence.detail: Audit found hard-coded empty state wording for style guide and glossary panels without matching reads in fetchDashboardData.

findings.impact: Reviewers can confuse unknown or not-yet-wired data with an authoritative empty state.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Dashboard panels distinguish unavailable, not-yet-wired, loading, empty, and populated states.
Style-guide and glossary panels use typed API data once the backing endpoints exist.
Tests cover unknown/unqueried state wording separately from true empty API responses.

proposedDagNode.title: Dashboard data-state precision
proposedDagNode.summary: Tighten dashboard panel states so style guide, glossary, jobs, and benchmark sections distinguish unknown, unavailable, empty, and populated API-backed states.
deliverables: Style guide and glossary populated-state tests
acceptanceCriteria: Panels never present unqueried data as confirmed empty state
Style guide and glossary panels render populated API-backed states when endpoints expose records
Loading, unavailable, empty, and populated states have distinct tests

verification.value: pnpm exec vp check apps/itotori/src apps/itotori/test

findings.title: Dashboard error rendering drops typed API errors
findings.description: Dashboard fetch failures report only route and status. Typed API error bodies and codes are not surfaced even though the server returns structured errors.

evidence.detail: Audit observed generic failed-to-load messages instead of typed API error code/body rendering.

findings.impact: Operators lose actionable remediation details during API failures.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Dashboard API errors render typed error code and message when the response body is available.
Error rendering safely handles malformed or missing error bodies.
MSW tests cover typed API error body rendering.

proposedDagNode.title: Typed dashboard API errors
proposedDagNode.summary: Render typed API error responses in the dashboard shell so failures show actionable code and message details while preserving safe fallback behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Typed API error code and message render when available
Malformed or missing error bodies fall back safely
Dashboard tests cover typed error and fallback error states
